criteria.name: Cash Flow Identification
criteria.description: All incremental cash flows correctly identified. Sunk costs excluded. Opportunity costs and side effects included.

scale.1: Major cash flows missing. Sunk costs included. Opportunity costs and side effects ignored. Cannot trust the analysis.
scale.3: Most cash flows identified. Sunk costs excluded. Some side effects may be missing. Working capital partially addressed.
scale.5: All incremental cash flows mapped. Sunk costs explicitly excluded with rationale. Opportunity costs quantified. Side effects (cannibalization, synergy) included. Working capital investment complete.

criteria.name: Discount Rate Appropriateness
criteria.description: Project-specific discount rate used when risk differs from company average. Country risk premium applied for international projects. Beta sourced from project's industry.

scale.1: Company WACC used without adjustment despite different project risk. No country risk consideration for international project. Beta mismatch.
scale.3: Some discount rate adjustment attempted. Country risk premium included but weighting may be imprecise. Beta from reasonable but not ideal comparables.
scale.5: Project-specific WACC correctly derived. Bottom-up beta from project industry peers. Revenue-weighted country risk premium for international exposure. Currency consistency between cash flows and discount rate.

criteria.name: NPV Calculation
criteria.description: NPV computed correctly with proper discounting mechanics. Initial investment at Time 0. Salvage value tax-adjusted and discounted.

scale.1: Math errors in discounting. Initial investment discounted incorrectly. Salvage value not tax-adjusted. Terminal value missing or wrong.
scale.3: NPV calculation mostly correct. Minor timing issues. Salvage value included but tax treatment approximate.
scale.5: NPV calculation accurate. Correct discounting at project-specific rate. Initial investment at Time 0. Salvage value tax-adjusted. All years accounted for. Depreciation tax shields correctly computed.

criteria.name: IRR Analysis
criteria.description: IRR computed and interpreted correctly. Limitations acknowledged when applicable (multiple IRRs, scale issues).

scale.1: IRR not computed, or computed incorrectly. No comparison to hurdle rate. Limitations not considered.
scale.3: IRR computed and compared to hurdle rate. Some awareness of limitations. Basic interpretation provided.
scale.5: IRR computed accurately. Compared to project-specific hurdle rate. Limitations explicitly addressed (non-conventional cash flows, scale problem if comparing projects). NPV used as primary decision metric with IRR as supplement.

criteria.name: ROC and EVA Assessment
criteria.description: Return on capital computed at steady state and compared to WACC. EVA quantified to show dollar value creation or destruction.

scale.1: No ROC or EVA analysis. Value creation not assessed beyond NPV.
scale.3: ROC computed and compared to WACC. EVA mentioned but not quantified or only for one year.
scale.5: ROC computed at steady state with clear invested capital definition. EVA quantified as (ROC - WACC) x Invested Capital. Ramp-up period acknowledged. Link between cumulative EVA and NPV explained.

criteria.name: Sensitivity Analysis
criteria.description: Key assumptions tested for impact on NPV and the investment decision. Breakeven values identified.

scale.1: No sensitivity analysis. Single-point estimate only. No understanding of which assumptions matter most.
scale.3: Sensitivity on 1-2 variables. Direction of impact noted. Some scenarios tested but breakeven not identified.
scale.5: Sensitivity on 3-5 key assumptions (market share, pricing, discount rate, costs, project life). Both one-variable and two-variable grids. Breakeven values identified. Robustness of decision across scenarios assessed.

criteria.name: Sunk and Opportunity Cost Treatment
criteria.description: Sunk costs explicitly excluded with rationale. Opportunity costs identified and included.

scale.1: Sunk costs included in investment. Opportunity costs ignored. No discussion of either concept.
scale.3: Sunk costs excluded. Opportunity costs mentioned but not fully quantified. Rationale brief.
scale.5: Sunk costs listed and explicitly excluded with clear rationale (already spent, unrecoverable). Opportunity costs identified, quantified, and included as project cash outflows. Side effects mapped comprehensively.

criteria.name: Recommendation Quality
criteria.description: Clear invest/defer/reject recommendation supported by NPV, IRR, ROC/EVA, and sensitivity results. Conditions and risks stated.

scale.1: No recommendation, or recommendation not supported by analysis. Disconnect between numbers and conclusion.
scale.3: Recommendation stated with basic support from NPV and IRR. Some conditions noted. Limited discussion of risks.
scale.5: Clear recommendation (invest/defer/reject) with specific conditions. Supported by NPV, IRR, ROC/EVA, and sensitivity analysis. Key risks identified. Trigger conditions stated for defer scenarios. Implementation considerations addressed.

common_pitfalls: Missing working capital investment during ramp-up
Using inflated revenue projections without sensitivity testing
Domestic Expansion.specific_guidance: Use company WACC. Focus on getting incremental cash flows right, especially cannibalization effects. Test sensitivity on market share and pricing assumptions.

common_pitfalls: Using company WACC without country risk adjustment
Mixing currencies between cash flows and discount rate
Ignoring political and regulatory risk in cash flow projections
International / Emerging Market Project.specific_guidance: Revenue-weighted country risk premium is essential. Ensure currency consistency. Test sensitivity on country risk premium and market penetration assumptions. Consider political and regulatory scenarios.

common_pitfalls: Confusing accounting profit with economic value creation
Not computing ROC correctly (using wrong invested capital base)
Sunk cost fallacy in continue/abandon decision
Existing Investment Assessment.specific_guidance: Focus on ROC vs WACC comparison and cumulative EVA. If ROC < WACC, the project destroys value even if profitable in accounting terms. The forward-looking question is whether to continue, not whether the original decision was right.

common_pitfalls: Choosing highest IRR instead of highest NPV
Not adjusting for different project lives
Mutually Exclusive Project Selection.specific_guidance: Rank by NPV for mutually exclusive projects. Use profitability index (NPV/Investment) under capital constraints. Compute equivalent annual NPV if project lives differ. Explicitly state why IRR ranking may differ from NPV ranking.

acceptable_shortcuts: Company WACC acceptable if same-risk project
IRR as primary metric acceptable for conventional cash flows
Simple (Single Cash Flow Stream, Domesti.specific_guidance: Focus on correctly identifying incremental cash flows and computing NPV. Ensure sunk costs excluded. Basic sensitivity on key revenue and cost assumptions.

acceptable_shortcuts: Regional CRP grouping rather than country-by-country
Standard (International, Multiple Revenu.specific_guidance: Full project-specific discount rate required. Map all revenue streams and cost categories. Include side effects. Sensitivity on 3+ variables. ROC/EVA assessment at steady state.

Complex (Multi-Region, Multi-Product, Ph.specific_guidance: Country-by-country risk adjustment. Phase-gated cash flows with decision points. Real options considerations for deferral value. Full sensitivity grid. Multiple scenarios (base, optimistic, pessimistic). Comparison to alternative projects if mutually exclusive.

common_failure_modes.name: Including Sunk Costs
common_failure_modes.symptom: Prior R&D or market research expenditures added to the initial investment, inflating the investment base and reducing NPV
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if any costs described as 'already spent' or 'already expensed' appear in the Time 0 outflow
common_failure_modes.fix: Remove all sunk costs from the cash flow table. List them separately with explanation of why they are excluded.

common_failure_modes.name: Missing Working Capital
common_failure_modes.symptom: Revenue grows but no corresponding investment in receivables, inventory, or payables. Cash flows overstated.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if working capital appears in cash flow table. If revenue grows 20% but working capital change is zero, likely missing.
common_failure_modes.fix: Model working capital as percentage of revenue or COGS. Compute change in working capital each year. Include as cash outflow when WC increases, cash inflow when WC decreases.

common_failure_modes.name: Wrong Discount Rate
common_failure_modes.symptom: Company-wide WACC used for a project with different risk profile. International project discounted at domestic rate.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if discount rate reflects project-specific risk. If project is in a different industry or geography but uses parent WACC, rate is wrong.
common_failure_modes.fix: Compute project-specific beta from industry peers. Add revenue-weighted country risk premium for international projects. Use project-specific WACC.

common_failure_modes.name: IRR Misused for Ranking
common_failure_modes.symptom: Small project with high IRR chosen over large project with higher NPV but lower IRR. Value left on table.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if mutually exclusive projects are ranked by IRR. If so, rerank by NPV.
common_failure_modes.fix: Use NPV as primary ranking metric. Report IRR as supplementary information. Explicitly note when IRR and NPV rankings diverge.

common_failure_modes.name: Accounting Profit Confused with Cash Flow
common_failure_modes.symptom: Net income used instead of free cash flow. Depreciation treated as cash outflow. Interest expense included in project cash flows.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if depreciation is subtracted in cash flow (it should be added back). Check if interest expense appears in project cash flows (it should not -- it is in WACC).
common_failure_modes.fix: Start with EBIT, apply taxes, add back depreciation, subtract CapEx and working capital changes. Do not include interest or financing flows.

common_failure_modes.name: Salvage Value Not Tax-Adjusted
common_failure_modes.symptom: Full salvage value included in final year without considering tax on gain or loss relative to book value.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if salvage value equals book value. If not, tax impact should be computed.
common_failure_modes.fix: After-tax Salvage = Salvage - Tax Rate x (Salvage - Book Value). If book value > salvage, the tax loss creates a shield.

common_failure_modes.name: No Sensitivity Analysis
common_failure_modes.symptom: Single-point NPV presented as the answer. No testing of what happens if key assumptions change.
common_failure_modes.detection: Ask: 'What market share makes NPV = 0?' If breakeven not computed, sensitivity is missing.
common_failure_modes.fix: Test 3-5 key assumptions. Compute breakeven values. Build two-variable sensitivity grid. Assess whether the decision is robust.

common_failure_modes.name: Missing Side Effects
common_failure_modes.symptom: Project analyzed in isolation without considering impact on existing business lines. Cannibalization or synergy ignored.
common_failure_modes.detection: Ask: 'Does this project affect sales of any existing product?' If yes and not in cash flows, side effects missing.
common_failure_modes.fix: Map all impacts on existing business. Include cannibalization as negative incremental cash flow. Include synergy as positive incremental cash flow. Be conservative on synergy estimates.

common_failure_modes.name: Double-Counting Depreciation
common_failure_modes.symptom: Depreciation subtracted as both a non-cash expense (in EBIT) and as a cash outflow. Cash flow understated.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if depreciation appears twice: once reducing EBIT and again as a separate cash outflow line.
common_failure_modes.fix: Depreciation reduces EBIT (which reduces taxes) but is added back to get cash flow. It is not a cash outflow. CapEx is the cash outflow; depreciation is its non-cash allocation over time.

common_failure_modes.name: Currency Mismatch
common_failure_modes.symptom: Cash flows in local currency discounted at USD rate, or USD cash flows discounted at local-currency rate. NPV systematically biased.
common_failure_modes.detection: Check if cash flow currency matches discount rate currency. If project is in India but discount rate is US WACC without country risk adjustment, there is a mismatch.
common_failure_modes.fix: Either convert cash flows to USD and discount at USD WACC + CRP, or keep cash flows in local currency and discount at local-currency rate. Both approaches should yield the same NPV.
